authoritative_sources.title: General Terms for Digital Advertising Agreements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB is the leading U.S. digital advertising trade association, and its general terms provide public industry contract workflow language for digital advertising agreements, order-specific terms, delivery, data, measurement, and dispute-sensitive client commitments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency-client delivery commitment, order, data-use, measurement, and dispute workflow
used_for: contractual exception and dispute escalation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence-backed service communication and escalation when a request touches digital advertising delivery commitments, data-use terms, disputes, or contractual exceptions.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IAB/4A's standard terms are widely used public agency-client-publisher workflow guidance for insertion orders, ad materials, placement delivery, late creative, makegoods, billing, cancellation, and data-use boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Insertion order, ad materials, campaign delivery, makegood, cancellation, billing, and client commitment workflow
used_for: late creative, delivery, makegood, billing, and cancellation escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the client-services lane from promising makegoods, billing treatment, or delivery outcomes without documented authority and owner review.

authoritative_sources.title: Agency Search Simplified
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. trade association for advertising agencies; its public agency-search guidance is authoritative for agency-client engagement setup, scope, stakeholder, procurement, and relationship workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency-client engagement intake, relationship setup, scope, and stakeholder alignment
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires client-service outputs to preserve engagement scope, stakeholder role, approval owner, and procurement or relationship context before making commitments.

authoritative_sources.title: Agency Compensation: A Guidebook for Marketers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ANA represents client-side marketers and publishes agency relationship and compensation guidance that informs scope, fee model, change-order, approval, and client commitment boundaries for agency services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client commitment, scope, fee model, change request, and service recovery boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces requests touching credits, makegoods, scope changes, fee treatment, or commitments to route to account, finance, legal, or contractual owners.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its online advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children-related issues that can require legal or policy review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated content, claims, disclosures, endorsements, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
safe communication boundaries for client questions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents client services from approving advertising claims, disclosures, endorsements, or regulated-content treatment while still allowing evidence packaging for legal or editorial review.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 255 Guides Concerning Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC Endorsement Guides are the public federal guidance baseline for endorsements, testimonials, material connections, clear disclosure, and advertiser responsibility, directly constraining agency responses about influencer or testimonial content.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Influencer, testimonial, endorsement, disclosure, and advertiser-responsibility boundary handling
used_for: client-service refusal for legal or editorial approval requests
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires influencer, testimonial, or review-content questions to be routed when approval or legal interpretation is requested.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising Substantiation Policy Statement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC substantiation policy is an official public policy statement on support for objective advertising claims, making it authoritative for refusing unsupported claim-approval or claim-defense requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claim support, substantiation evidence, and unsupported-claim escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from converting client-service status handling into claims substantiation or legal approval.

authoritative_sources.title: PRSA Code of Ethics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PRSA's public ethics code is a recognized communications-profession framework for accuracy, disclosure, conflicts, competition, and safeguarding confidences, directly relevant to agency client-service communications and handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accuracy, disclosure, conflict, and confidentiality-aware communication handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires client-service messages to be accurate, evidence-based, caveated, and confidentiality-safe before delivery.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 10002 is the recognized international standard for complaints handling in organizations and is authoritative for complaint intake, acknowledgement, investigation, response, escalation, and records discipline in service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint intake, service recovery, repeated failure, escalation, and closure evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains complaint and service recovery scenarios to record evidence, preserve ownership, and escalate beyond documented authority.

workflow_stages.name: Client-service intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, brand, request type, campaign or project, MSA, SOW, IO, stakeholder role, communication channel, authority matrix, confidentiality constraints, and systems of record before responding.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for client, engagement, content, and delivery records
workflow_stages.description: Assemble CRM cases, tickets, project plans, client briefs, campaign briefs, content assets, review notes, QA checks, approvals, delivery records, change requests, and communication history into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution or blocker classification
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether evidence supports direct resolution, missing-prerequisite handling, clarification, complaint classification, repeat-failure escalation, owner transfer, or boundary refusal.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Stakeholder communication preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare communication summaries using approved facts, caveats, documented commitments, source references, safe next steps, approved recipients, and approved channels without overstating authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and owner-transfer escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route unresolved policy exceptions, makegood or credit requests, repeated failures, claim or disclosure questions, rights or creative approval issues, source conflicts, confidentiality holds, and contractual signoff dependencies to the correct owner.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and recordkeeping
workflow_stages.description: Capture action taken, evidence checked, approved communication, unresolved items, next owner, escalation rationale, client-impact state, and retention location before marking a request resolved or transferred.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Master service agreement, statement of work, agency-of-record scope, insertion order, client commitments, service policy, approval matrix, confidentiality restrictions, and escalation thresholds
Client file, CRM account, ticket or case, project plan, status tracker, client brief, campaign brief, creative brief, media plan, content asset ids, and delivery timeline
Content assets, proofs, review notes, legal or editorial comments, QA checks, redlines, version history, approval records, acceptance evidence, and proof of performance
Change requests, makegood or credit escalation records, service recovery notes, complaint history, issue logs, repeat-failure indicators, and prior owner-transfer notes
Client communication logs, meeting notes, stakeholder recipient list, approved communication channel, delivery confirmation, unresolved-item log, and retention location
Adjacent-owner handoff packages for legal, editorial, quality, delivery, account, media, finance, privacy, creative, or contractual signoff owners
systems_of_record: document management and client file repository
content and digital asset management system
ad operations, media planning, ad server, publisher, or platform systems where relevant
delivery, proof-of-performance, analytics, and reporting systems
finance, billing, estimate, PO, and change-order systems where relevant
client communication, secure delivery, audit log, retention, and data-security systems

allowed: Classify a request as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, exception-driven, complaint-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive using documented source records.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, missing-prerequisite notes, escalation packages, and owner-transfer handoffs.
Confirm documented workflow state, approval state, missing artifacts, next steps, owner, due date, and unresolved items without changing contractual or approval posture.
Apply documented service, communication, confidentiality, escalation, and recordkeeping rules within tenant-granted authority.
escalate: Tenant policy, client commitment, MSA, SOW, IO, approval owner, project record, jurisdiction, channel, platform policy, or system record is missing and would change the answer.
Client instructions, tenant policy, contract terms, public guidance, platform or publisher policy, legal comments, and retrieved records materially conflict.
The request touches claims, substantiation, endorsements, disclosures, regulated content, privacy, audience data, rights, final creative approval, or publication authority.
The request asks for a makegood, credit, refund, compensation, waiver, budget change, scope change, fee treatment, policy exception, or service recovery outside documented authority.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-content, client-file, audience-data, pricing, performance-data, platform-credential, or agency-work-product conflict affects communication or delivery.
Repeat failures, unresolved QA defects, client harm, professional judgment, client rights, or final acceptance determine the next step.
refuse: Requests to approve legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, creative concepts, privacy-law interpretation, targeting strategy, media strategy, budgets, makegoods, credits, refunds, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.
Requests to hide known blockers, omit QA failures, imply approval, promise resolution, or close a request when source records, approvals, or authority are missing.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, or send client files, audience data, performance data, pricing, platform credentials, unreleased content, or agency work product outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a vague client email or informal internal note as sufficient evidence for a client-facing promise.
Closing a client-service request without SOW, project, asset, approval, QA, delivery, communication, or retention evidence.
Approving a claim, endorsement, disclosure, final creative asset, rights posture, or privacy posture from a service lane.
Promising a makegood, credit, refund, fee waiver, budget change, scope change, or service recovery without documented authority.
Suppressing known QA defects, missed milestones, open legal comments, conflicting owner notes, or unresolved client-impact facts to avoid escalation.
Disclosing client files, unreleased creative, campaign performance, pricing, audience segments, platform credentials, or agency work product through an unauthorized recipient or channel.
Flattening agency client services into generic customer support without MSA, SOW, AOR, IO, project, campaign, content, approval, and agency relationship context.
Resolving conflicting client instructions, contract terms, tenant policy, platform policy, and legal review comments by guessing instead of escalating.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific agency authority and client-service model
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline does not grant account-management, financial, contractual, editorial, legal, media strategy, or final client signoff authority. Tenant MSA, SOW, policy, approval matrix, and retrieval hooks must be supplied before case-specific resolution.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Channel, platform, publisher, and regulated-vertical differences
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats channel-specific platform rules, regulated-vertical controls, privacy obligations, and publisher policies as tenant/runtime prerequisites and escalation triggers when they affect the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional advertising, privacy, and consumer-protection interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane uses FTC and public professional baselines for issue recognition but routes legal, privacy, rights, claims, disclosures, and regulated-content interpretation to authorized owners.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, complaint handling, client-delivery artifacts, confidentiality, advertising claim boundaries, and failure handling using IAB, 4A's, ANA, FTC, PRSA, and ISO sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when tenant policy, source records, engagement scope, approval authority, confidentiality, legal, editorial, privacy, contractual, financial, or final signoff facts are missing.
